title: Lambertia elegans
taxonomy: Lambertia elegans is a species of flowering plant in the family Proteaceae. This classification places Lambertia elegans within the order Proteales. The family Proteaceae is a large group of flowering plants native to the Southern Hemisphere. Lambertia elegans is native to Australia, and its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other Proteaceae species. Its classification also indicates its similarity to other plants in the Proteales order, such as the genus Stenocarpus.

intro: Lambertia elegans, commonly known as the mountain devil or purple cornflower, is a flowering plant native to Western Australia. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, coughs, and skin irritations, due to its antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties.
